dataset_name: WPA Protocol Symbols Lab — Combined Dataset (Pilot + Phase 2A)
approved_by: Pilot 5 approved by Director 23 May 2026; Phase 2A pending Director approval
doctrine: Преговарањето е опционално. Протоколот е апсолутен.

flag.description_en: A red field with a stylized yellow sun in the centre, with eight broadening rays extending from the central disc to the edges of the field.
flag.design_law: Закон за знамето на Република Македонија (Службен весник на РМ, бр. 47/1995); Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia, Article 5.

anthem.design_law: Закон за химната на Република Македонија (1992).

coat_of_arms.description_en: Two curved garlands of sheaves of wheat, tobacco leaves and opium poppy fruits, tied by a ribbon decorated with traditional Macedonian folk embroidery. Within the ovoid frame: a mountain, a lake and a rising sun. Red star removed in 2009.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia, Article 5; amended 2009 to remove socialist star.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот, авторот и пропорциите на знамето. Споредна контрадикција: повеќето извори (FOTW, worldflag.org, CIA-State.gov, Wikipedia/EN) посочуваат датум на усвојување 5 октомври 1995. Еден извор (Grokipedia, упатувајќи се на Сл. весник 47/95) посочува 5 септември 1995 како датум на објавување. WPA го прифаќа 5 октомври 1995 како канонски датум на усвојување (consistent across majority of sources), но го документира контрадикторниот извор. Препорачана дипломатска корекција од МНР на РСМ за финална потврда.

sources.name: Викиизвор — Закон за знамето на Република Македонија (тековен текст)
US Department of State (citing CIA World Factbook) — Macedonia Flag
National Bank of the Republic of North Macedonia (official institutional reference); Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia
Flags of the World (FOTW) — North Macedonia

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: Овој запис е предмет на активен дипломатски дијалог: WPA подготвува формално барање за потврда од Министерството за надворешни работи на Република Северна Македонија за прецизна разлика помеѓу датумот на усвојување (5 октомври 1995) и датумот на објавување во „Службен весник на Република Македонија" бр. 47/1995. Канонски датум за WPA е 5 октомври 1995 до конечна државна потврда.

flag.description_en: Thirteen equal horizontal stripes of red (top and bottom) alternating with white; a blue rectangle in the upper hoist-side corner bears 50 small white five-pointed stars arranged in nine offset horizontal rows of six stars (top and bottom) alternating with rows of five stars. The 50 stars represent the 50 states; the 13 stripes represent the 13 original colonies.
flag.design_law: Flag Act of 1818 (4 U.S.C. § 1); Executive Order 10834 of 21 August 1959, fixing the 50-star design effective 4 July 1960.

anthem.composer: John Stafford Smith (melody, originally 'To Anacreon in Heaven')
anthem.design_law: Act of Congress, 3 March 1931 (46 Stat. 1508), codified at 36 U.S.C. § 301.

coat_of_arms.description_en: Great Seal of the United States. Obverse: a bald eagle with a shield of 13 paleways argent and gules, a chief azure; in its dexter talon an olive branch, in its sinister thirteen arrows; in its beak a scroll inscribed 'E PLURIBUS UNUM'. Above the eagle's head: a glory breaking through a cloud surrounding 13 stars.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Resolution of the Continental Congress, 20 June 1782; codified at 4 U.S.C. § 41.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (CIA World Factbook, U.S. State Department, 4 U.S.C. кодификација) се целосно согласни на сите јадрени полиња.

sources.name: CIA World Factbook — United States (Flag)
4 U.S.C. § 1 et seq. (U.S. Flag Code); 36 U.S.C. § 301 (National Anthem)
U.S. Department of State — Great Seal of the United States

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

flag.description_en: Red field with a large yellow five-pointed star and four smaller yellow five-pointed stars (arranged in a vertical arc toward the centre of the flag) in the upper hoist-side corner. Red represents the revolution; the stars symbolise the four social classes united under the Communist Party of China, with the larger star representing the leadership of the CCP.
flag.design_law: Resolution of the First Plenary Session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, 27 September 1949; Law of the People's Republic of China on the National Flag, adopted 1990, amended 2009 and 2020; Constitution of the PRC (1982, amended 2018), Article 141 (Chapter IV).

anthem.design_law: Adopted provisionally 27 September 1949 by the CPPCC; formalised in the Constitution of the PRC 1982 (Article 141); Law of the PRC on the National Anthem, 1 October 2017 (effective).

coat_of_arms.description_en: National emblem of the PRC: Tiananmen Gate beneath five stars (one large, four small in arc) in gold, framed below by a gear and encircled by ears of grain — all in gold on a red disc.
coat_of_arms.designer: Lin Huiyin and design team, Tsinghua University.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Adopted by the Central People's Government Council, 20 September 1950; Constitution of the PRC, Article 142.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се целосно согласни. Забелешка: CIA Factbook 2023 archive го документира националниот симбол; PRC Constitution (Chapter IV) ги дава уставните рамки; NPC дава полна правна структура.

sources.name: Constitution of the People's Republic of China (1982, amended 2018), Chapter IV; Law of the PRC on the National Flag (1990, am. 2020).
CIA World Factbook — China
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China — National Symbols

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

capital.notes: Bonn served as the federal seat of government 1949–1999 before the federal institutions moved to Berlin; Berlin is the constitutional capital under Article 22(1) of the Basic Law.

flag.description_en: Three equal horizontal bands of black (top), red (middle), and gold (bottom). Proportion 3:5. The colours trace back to the uniforms of German soldiers during the Napoleonic Wars and were established by the Weimar Republic; readopted by the Federal Republic in 1949.
flag.design_law: Grundgesetz für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland (Basic Law), Article 22(2): 'The federal flag shall be black, red and gold.' (Promulgated 23 May 1949; design first established 9 May 1949 by the Parliamentary Council.)
flag.designer: Tradition (Weimar Republic, 1919, after the colours of the Lützow Free Corps, 1813).

anthem.title_local: Deutschlandlied (Das Lied der Deutschen) — dritte Strophe
anthem.title_en: Song of Germany — third stanza only
anthem.design_law: Exchange of letters between Federal President Richard von Weizsäcker and Federal Chancellor Helmut Kohl, 19 August 1991, confirming the third stanza as the national anthem of the reunified Germany. (Original West German adoption: exchange of letters Theodor Heuss / Konrad Adenauer, 2 May 1952.)

coat_of_arms.description_en: Bundesadler (Federal Eagle): a single-headed black eagle with open wings, red beak and red feet, on a blazoned golden-yellow shield. Based on the eagle of the Weimar Republic (1928), itself derived from the medieval imperial eagle.
coat_of_arms.designer: Tobias Schwab (Weimar version, 1928); confirmed 20 January 1950 by Federal President Theodor Heuss.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Bekanntmachung des Bundespräsidenten, 20 January 1950 (Federal Law Gazette I, p. 26).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот, бојата и хералдичкиот опис. Споредна контрадикција околу датум на усвојување на знамето: некои извори (FOTW) посочуваат 9 мај 1949 (датум на одредба од Parlamentarischer Rat), додека WorldAtlas и официјалниот BMI извор го посочуваат 23 мај 1949 (датумот на објавување на Grundgesetz). WPA го прифаќа 23 мај 1949 како канонски датум (датум на ефективност на Уставот), со 9 мај 1949 документиран како adoption_date_first.

sources.name: Bundesministerium des Innern und für Heimat (BMI) — Germany's State Symbols (official)
CIA World Factbook — Germany
Grundgesetz für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland, Article 22 (Federal Law Gazette)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: This record is subject to active diplomatic dialogue: WPA is preparing a formal request to the Bundesministerium des Innern und für Heimat (BMI) for confirmation of the canonical adoption date of the Federal flag. WPA currently treats 23 May 1949 (promulgation of the Grundgesetz) as canonical; 9 May 1949 (vote of the Parliamentary Council) is preserved as adoption_date_first.

capital.notes: Capital since 21 April 1960 (previously Rio de Janeiro).

flag.description_en: A green field with a large yellow rhombus in the centre, bearing a blue disc which forms a celestial globe depicting 27 small white five-pointed stars spanned by a white equatorial curved band with the national motto 'Ordem e Progresso' (Order and Progress) written in green. The 27 stars represent the 26 federal states and the Federal District; their positions reproduce the southern sky over Rio de Janeiro on the morning of the Proclamation of the Republic, 15 November 1889.
flag.design_law: Decreto nº 4, 19 November 1889 (original design); Lei nº 8.421, signed 11 May 1992 by President Fernando Collor de Mello, published in Diário Oficial da União 12 May 1992 (current 27-star configuration). Per WPA editorial decision (Sande, 23 May 2026), the canonical adoption date is the date of official publication (12 May 1992); the signing date (11 May 1992) is retained in this design_law note as historical reference.
flag.designer: Raimundo Teixeira Mendes (with Manuel Pereira Reis, Miguel Lemos, Décio Villares).

anthem.design_law: Decreto nº 171, 20 January 1890 (melody made official); Decreto nº 15.671, 6 September 1922 (current lyrics officially adopted by Duque-Estrada); Lei nº 5.700, 1 September 1971 (consolidated law on national symbols).

coat_of_arms.description_en: A circular blue shield bearing the Southern Cross constellation on a white star-studded field, surrounded by a green border with 27 silver stars (representing the 26 states and the Federal District). The shield rests on a five-pointed yellow star with rays. Two branches frame the composition: coffee on the dexter side and tobacco on the sinister side. Below: a blue ribbon inscribed 'REPÚBLICA FEDERATIVA DO BRASIL / 15 DE NOVEMBRO DE 1889'.
coat_of_arms.designer: Artur Zauer (original design); updated by Lei 8.421/1992.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Lei nº 5.700, 1 September 1971; Lei nº 8.421, signed 11 May 1992, published in Diário Oficial da União 12 May 1992 (current configuration).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот и бројот на ѕвезди (27). Споредна разлика околу датумот на тековното знаме: повеќето извори (CIA Factbook, Wikipedia, текст на Lei 8.421) посочуваат 11 мај 1992 (датум на потпишување на законот од Претседателот Collor). Еден извор (worldflag.org) посочува 12 мај 1992 (датум на објавување во Diário Oficial da União). Per WPA Director editorial decision (Sande, 23 May 2026), WPA го прифаќа 12 мај 1992 (датум на објавување) како канонски датум на адопција, со фуснота за потписот од 11 мај 1992.

sources.name: Lei nº 8.421, 11 May 1992 — Presidência da República, Casa Civil (Planalto)
CIA World Factbook — Brazil
Lei nº 5.700 de 1 de setembro de 1971 — Símbolos Nacionais (Planalto)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: WPA editorial decision (Director, 23 May 2026): The canonical adoption date for the current 27-star flag is recorded as 12 May 1992 (Diário Oficial da União publication of Lei nº 8.421). The signing date of 11 May 1992 by President Fernando Collor de Mello is documented as a historical footnote in the design_law field.

records.official_name_en: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

official_name_local.en: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

flag.description_en: The Union Jack (Union Flag): a blue field bearing the red cross of Saint George (England) fimbriated in white, overlaid on the diagonal white saltire of Saint Andrew (Scotland) counterchanged with the diagonal red saltire of Saint Patrick (Ireland). Composite symbol of the union of the kingdoms.
flag.design_law: The Union Flag was established by Royal Proclamation of 12 April 1606 (Great Britain version); the present design was established by Royal Proclamation following the Acts of Union 1800 (effective 1 January 1801). The Union Jack has become the national flag by precedent rather than by a single Act of Parliament.

anthem.lyricist: Unknown (traditional; sometimes attributed to John Bull)
anthem.design_law: Adopted by convention; first published in The Gentleman's Magazine, October 1745. No statutory enactment — the anthem holds its status by long-standing royal and parliamentary precedent.

coat_of_arms.description_en: The Royal Coat of Arms of the United Kingdom: quarterly, 1st and 4th gules three lions passant guardant in pale or (England); 2nd or a lion rampant within a double tressure flory counter-flory gules (Scotland); 3rd azure a harp or stringed argent (Ireland). Supporters: a lion rampant guardant or imperially crowned (dexter), a unicorn argent armed, crined and unguled or (sinister). Motto on the Garter: 'Honi soit qui mal y pense'; below: 'Dieu et mon droit'.
coat_of_arms.designer: Royal heralds — College of Arms (current form established under Queen Victoria 1837).
coat_of_arms.design_law: Royal Warrant of Queen Victoria, 1837 (current Royal Arms); modifications by subsequent monarchs as needed.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот, главниот град и химната. Споредна правна особеност: знамето е национален симбол по преседан, не по поединечен парламентарен акт — има многу референци за 1 јануари 1801 (Acts of Union ефективен датум) но нема единствен 'Flag Act of 1801'. Wikipedia/Union Jack ова експлицитно го забележува. WPA го прифаќа 1 јануари 1801 како канонски датум поради ефективноста на Унијата.

sources.name: Acts of Union 1800 (39 & 40 Geo 3 c 67; legislation.gov.uk)
CIA World Factbook — United Kingdom
The Royal Household — National Anthem; Royal Coat of Arms (royal.uk)
The Flag Institute — Union Jack

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: The Tricolore: a vertical triband of blue (hoist), white, and red, in equal widths. Designed during the French Revolution; blue and red are the traditional colours of the City of Paris, white the colour of the monarchy and of France.
flag.design_law: Decree of the National Convention, 27 Pluviôse Year II (15 February 1794); constitutional status under Article 2 of the Constitution of 4 October 1958 (Fifth Republic): 'L'emblème national est le drapeau tricolore, bleu, blanc, rouge.'
flag.designer: Jacques-Louis David (commissioned by the National Convention).

anthem.design_law: Decree of the National Convention, 14 July 1795 (26 Messidor Year III); readopted 1879; constitutional status under Article 2 of the Constitution of 4 October 1958: 'L'hymne national est La Marseillaise.'

coat_of_arms.description_en: France has no de jure coat of arms. The unofficial emblem of the French Republic, used by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and on French passports since 1953, features a lictor's fasces bearing the axe of authority, surrounded by laurel and oak branches; an oval shield bearing the letters 'RF' (République Française); ribbons inscribed 'Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité'.
coat_of_arms.designer: Robert Louis (graphic artist, 1953 design for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs).
coat_of_arms.design_law: No constitutional or statutory enactment; the emblem is used by administrative convention. The Constitution of 1958 (Article 2) recognises the flag, anthem, and motto as official symbols, but not a coat of arms.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три (+ supplementary europa.eu и Quirinale-equivalent presidential) извори се потполно согласни на знамето, химната и главниот град. Грбот е јасно означен како незваничен — ова не е контрадикција туку правна реалност на Француската Република.

sources.name: Constitution of the Fifth French Republic, Article 2 (4 October 1958)
CIA World Factbook — France
Élysée — Symboles de la République française (elysee.fr)
europa.eu — France (EU Member State page)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: Il Tricolore: a vertical triband of green (hoist), white, and red, in equal widths. Origin in the Cispadane Republic (1797); adopted by the Italian Republic upon entry into force of the Constitution.
flag.design_law: Constitution of the Italian Republic, Article 12 (entry into force 1 January 1948): 'La bandiera della Repubblica è il tricolore italiano: verde, bianco e rosso, a tre bande verticali di eguali dimensioni.' Current colour specifications: Presidency of the Council Directive, 14 April 2006.
flag.designer: Tradition (Cispadane Republic, 7 January 1797 — proposed by deputy Giuseppe Compagnoni at Reggio Emilia).

anthem.title_local: Il Canto degli Italiani (Inno di Mameli)
anthem.title_en: The Song of the Italians (Mameli's Hymn)
anthem.design_law: Provisional adoption: Decree of the Council of Ministers, 12 October 1946. De jure adoption: Law No. 181 of 4 December 2017, recognising 'Il Canto degli Italiani' as the official national anthem.

coat_of_arms.description_en: Emblem of the Italian Republic (Emblema della Repubblica Italiana): a white five-pointed star with red border (Stella d'Italia) superimposed on a five-spoked steel cogwheel (representing labour, Article 1 of the Constitution), framed by an olive branch (dexter, symbolising peace) and an oak branch (sinister, symbolising strength). Below: a red ribbon inscribed 'REPVBBLICA ITALIANA' in gold.
coat_of_arms.designer: Paolo Paschetto (winner of national competition, 1946; revised 1948).
coat_of_arms.design_law: Decreto Legislativo del Capo provvisorio dello Stato, 5 May 1948, No. 535. (Originally established 31 January 1948.)

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (Quirinale, CIA Factbook, Constitution Art. 12, supplementary europa.eu) се потполно согласни на сите јадрени полиња. Историскиот датум 1797-01-07 (Cispadane Republic) е документиран како adoption_date_first.

sources.name: Constitution of the Italian Republic, Article 12; Law No. 181/2017 on the National Anthem
CIA World Factbook — Italy
Presidenza della Repubblica Italiana — Quirinale (quirinale.it)
europa.eu — Italy (EU Member State page)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: La Rojigualda: three horizontal stripes — red, yellow, and red — with the yellow stripe twice the width of each red stripe. The state flag bears the Spanish coat of arms positioned on the yellow stripe, off-centre toward the hoist.
flag.design_law: Constitution of Spain (1978), Article 4(1): 'La bandera de España está formada por tres franjas horizontales, roja, amarilla y roja, siendo la amarilla de doble anchura que cada una de las rojas.' Law 39/1981 of 28 October 1981, regulating the use of the flag; Royal Decree 441/1981 of 27 February 1981, defining the colours technically; Royal Decree 2964/1981 of 18 December 1981, establishing the coat of arms on the flag.
flag.designer: Antonio Valdés y Bazán (original 1785 naval ensign design).

anthem.composer: Anonymous (traditional; first published in Manuel de Espinosa de los Monteros, 'Libro de la Ordenanza de los Toques de Pífanos y Tambores que se tocan nuevamente en la Ynfantª Española', 1761). Current arrangement by Francisco Grau Vegara.
anthem.design_law: Royal Decree 1560/1997 of 10 October 1997, regulating the National Anthem (currently authoritative version).

coat_of_arms.description_en: The arms of Spain: a quartered shield of the historic kingdoms — Castile (gules, a castle or, masoned sable and ajouré azure), León (argent, a lion rampant purpure crowned or), Aragon (or, four pallets gules), Navarre (gules, chains or in cross, saltire and orle, with an emerald in centre); enté en point: a pomegranate (Granada). Royal crown atop. Flanked by the Pillars of Hercules with crowns and the motto 'PLUS ULTRA' on a red scroll. Central inescutcheon: arms of the House of Bourbon-Anjou (azure, three fleurs-de-lis or, with a red border).
coat_of_arms.designer: Manuel Galiana (graphic design); heraldic redesign committee 1981.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Law 33/1981 of 5 October 1981, on the Spanish Coat of Arms (Escudo de España).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (La Moncloa, Exteriores, CIA Factbook + supplementary europa.eu) се потполно согласни на сите јадрени полиња. Спанскиот правен пакет (Закон 39/1981 за знамето, Закон 33/1981 за грбот, Декрет 1560/1997 за химната) е најкомплетниот од сите досегашни записи во базата.

sources.name: Constitution of Spain (1978), Article 4; Law 33/1981 (Coat of Arms); Law 39/1981 (Flag); Royal Decree 1560/1997 (Anthem)
CIA World Factbook — Spain
La Moncloa (Government of Spain) — State Symbols (lamoncloa.gob.es)
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Spain — Protocol (exteriores.gob.es)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: The National Flag of Canada (the Maple Leaf, l'Unifolié): a vertical triband of red (hoist), white (double width central pale), and red (fly), with a stylised red eleven-pointed maple leaf charged in the centre. Heraldic blazon: 'Gules on a Canadian pale Argent a maple leaf Gules'.
flag.design_law: Royal Proclamation of Queen Elizabeth II, signed 28 January 1965, declaring the flag effective 15 February 1965. Canadian Heraldic Authority (created 1988) currently administers official heraldry.
flag.designer: George F. G. Stanley (concept); John Matheson (parliamentary champion); final design by Jacques St. Cyr.

anthem.lyricist: Adolphe-Basile Routhier (French original, 1880); Robert Stanley Weir (English version, 1908)
anthem.design_law: National Anthem Act, S.C. 1980, c. 5, assented to 27 June 1980, proclaimed in force 1 July 1980 (Canada Day).

coat_of_arms.description_en: The Royal Arms of Canada (Arms of His Majesty in Right of Canada): a shield quartered — 1st, three lions passant guardant in pale or (England); 2nd, a lion rampant within a double tressure flory-counter-flory gules (Scotland); 3rd, a harp or stringed argent (Ireland); 4th, three fleurs-de-lis or (Royal Arms of France); base, a sprig of three maple leaves vert (Canada). Above: royal helm and crown, with a crowned lion holding a maple leaf. Supporters: English lion (dexter) and Scottish unicorn (sinister) bearing the Union Jack and the Royal Banner of France. Motto: 'A MARI USQUE AD MARE' (From Sea to Sea). Above on red ribbon: 'DESIDERANTES MELIOREM PATRIAM'.
coat_of_arms.designer: Original: Royal Warrant of King George V, 21 November 1921. Current: revised by Order in Council 1994 (motto ribbon added).
coat_of_arms.design_law: Royal Proclamation of 21 November 1921 (King George V); modified by Order in Council, 12 July 1994.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (canada.ca, CIA Factbook, Canadian Heraldic Authority) се потполно согласни. Канада има два прифатени датуми за знамето: 28 јануари 1965 (royal proclamation signing) и 15 февруари 1965 (effective date / first raising on Parliament Hill). WPA го прифаќа 15 февруари 1965 како канонски (effective date, National Flag of Canada Day) во согласност со официјалниот канадски пристап.

sources.name: National Anthem Act (S.C. 1980, c. 5); Royal Proclamation 1965 (Canada Gazette)
CIA World Factbook — Canada
Government of Canada — Official Symbols of Canada (canada.ca)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: A dark blue (Pantone 280) field with the Union Jack in the upper hoist quarter (canton); a large white seven-pointed Commonwealth (Federation) Star below the Union Jack, with six points for the six states and a seventh for the territories; in the fly, the Southern Cross constellation depicted as five white stars (four seven-pointed and one smaller five-pointed star — Epsilon Crucis).
flag.design_law: Flags Act 1953 (Cth), assented to 14 February 1954 by Royal Assent of Queen Elizabeth II; established the design as the 'Australian National Flag'. Earlier seven-pointed Commonwealth Star version officially gazetted 19 February 1908.
flag.designer: Selected by competition 1901 (joint design: Annie Dorrington, Ivor Evans, Leslie Hawkins, Egbert Nuttall, William Stevens).

anthem.lyricist: Peter Dodds McCormick (1878; modified 2021 — 'For we are one and free')
anthem.design_law: Proclamation by Governor-General Sir Ninian Stephen, 19 April 1984, made on the advice of the Hawke Government; lyric amendment 'one and free' proclaimed 1 January 2021.

coat_of_arms.description_en: Coat of Arms of Australia (Commonwealth Arms): a shield of six quarters bearing the badges of the six states — 1st, New South Wales (red cross with lion and four stars); 2nd, Victoria (white cross with Southern Cross and crown); 3rd, Queensland (Maltese cross with crown); 4th, South Australia (piping shrike); 5th, Western Australia (black swan); 6th, Tasmania (red lion). Shield bordered with ermine signifying federation. Above: a seven-pointed gold Commonwealth Star on a blue-and-gold wreath. Supporters: red kangaroo (dexter) and emu (sinister) standing on a grassy mound with golden wattle. Below: scroll 'AUSTRALIA'.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Royal Warrant of King George V, 19 September 1912 (current Arms); preceded by Royal Warrant of King Edward VII, 7 May 1908.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (pmc.gov.au, CIA Factbook, ANFA / National Museum of Australia) се потполно согласни на сите јадрени полиња. Минор детал: knigата ги документира двата датуми — 8 декември 1908 (грб со 7-точкаст Commonwealth Star) и 14 февруари 1954 (Flags Act effective). WPA го прифаќа 14 февруари 1954 како канонски датум за статутарно усвојување на знамето.

sources.name: Flags Act 1953 (Cth) — Federal Register of Legislation
CIA World Factbook — Australia
Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet — Australian National Symbols (pmc.gov.au)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

capital.notes: Amsterdam is the constitutional capital under Article 32 of the Constitution. The seat of government — the States General, the Royal Family's principal residences, the Council of Ministers, and the Supreme Court — is located in The Hague (Den Haag). The distinction is constitutionally clear: Amsterdam is the capital, The Hague is the seat of government.

flag.description_en: A horizontal triband of bright vermilion red (top), white (middle), and cobalt blue (bottom), in equal widths. Considered the oldest tricolour flag in continuous use (derived from the Statenvlag of 1630, in turn derived from the Prinsenvlag of 1572).
flag.design_law: Royal Decree of Queen Wilhelmina, 19 February 1937, confirming the colours red, white, blue (rather than the historic orange-white-blue Prinsenvlag) as the official Dutch flag.

anthem.lyricist: Possibly Philips of Marnix, Lord of Saint-Aldegonde (attributed; c. 1568–1572)
anthem.composer: Adriaen Valerius (arrangement, 1626); melody from a French Huguenot soldier's song
anthem.design_law: Royal Decree of Queen Wilhelmina, 10 May 1932, officially adopting 'Wilhelmus' as the national anthem of the Netherlands.

coat_of_arms.description_en: Royal Coat of Arms of the Kingdom of the Netherlands: azure, billetty or, a lion rampant or, armed and langued gules, holding in its dexter paw a sword argent hilted or and in its sinister paw a sheaf of seven arrows or (representing the seven provinces of the Union of Utrecht 1579). The shield is crowned with the Dutch royal crown and supported by two lions or, armed and langued gules. Below: a scroll azure inscribed in or with the motto 'JE MAINTIENDRAI' (French: 'I will maintain'). The monarch's variant adds a red ermine-lined mantle topped by a royal pavilion.
coat_of_arms.designer: Royal heraldic authority of the Netherlands.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Royal Decree of Queen Wilhelmina, 23 August 1907 (current design and proportions).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (Rijksoverheid, CIA Factbook, Royal House of the Netherlands + supplementary europa.eu) се потполно согласни. Капиталот е документиран со посебна notes-белешка за тоа што Хаг е седиштето на владата (правна разлика добро позната во меѓународниот протокол).

sources.name: Constitution of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, Article 32; Royal Decree of 19 February 1937 (Staatsblad)
CIA World Factbook — Netherlands
Rijksoverheid (Government of the Netherlands) — Nederlandse vlag
europa.eu — Netherlands (EU Member State page)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (Phase 2A)

flag.description_en: A vertical triband of black (hoist), yellow (middle), and red (fly). The colours are derived from the arms of the historic Duchy of Brabant. Vertical orientation adopted to distinguish from the Dutch horizontal tricolour.
flag.design_law: Decree of the Provisional Government of Belgium, 23 January 1831 (establishing vertical orientation with black at the hoist); constitutional status under Article 193 of the Belgian Constitution: 'La nation belge choisit pour couleurs le rouge, le jaune et le noir, et pour armes le Lion belge avec la légende: L'UNION FAIT LA FORCE.' (Originally Article 125.) Official proportion 13:15; civil ensign use 2:3 also accepted.
flag.designer: Tradition (colours from the Duchy of Brabant; vertical arrangement: Provisional Government 1831).

anthem.title_local: La Brabançonne / De Brabançonne / Das Lied von Brabant
anthem.lyricist: Alexandre Dechet ('Jenneval', 1830); revised by Charles Rogier 1860 (French version); Dutch and German official versions added later.
anthem.design_law: Adopted by convention 1830; current standardised text and arrangement established 1921; trilingual official versions confirmed by Ministerial Circular of 8 August 1921.

coat_of_arms.description_en: The Belgian Lion (Leo Belgicus): sable, a lion rampant or, armed and langued gules (a golden lion on black, with red claws and tongue), based on the arms of the Duchy of Brabant. The Greater Royal Coat of Arms shows the shield surmounted by a royal crown, supported by two lions rampant or, holding banners of the nine historic provinces (1837); the whole behind crossed sceptres on a red mantle lined with ermine; motto on scroll: 'L'UNION FAIT LA FORCE' / 'EENDRACHT MAAKT MACHT' / 'EINIGKEIT MACHT STARK' (Unity Makes Strength).
coat_of_arms.design_law: Royal Decree of King Leopold I, 17 May 1837 (Greater Royal Arms); constitutional basis: Article 193 of the Constitution of Belgium.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (Belgian Senate constitutional database, CIA Factbook, FPS Foreign Affairs + supplementary europa.eu) се потполно согласни. Знамето има интересна правна особеност: уставната пропорција 13:15 е официјална, додека во цивилна употреба 2:3 е широко прифатена — двата случаи се документирани.

sources.name: Constitution of Belgium, Article 193 (Belgian Senate SenLex database)
CIA World Factbook — Belgium
Belgium.be — Belgian Federal Authorities, National Symbols
europa.eu — Belgium (EU Member State page; Belgium is founding EU member)
